summary: Set 9 Session 2 (D-1): Resolved (repo, branch) parallel-session exclusion via the audit-accepted doc-only alternative (option b). Revised the original proposal's Open questions (revised) Q2 to lead with the shipping contract (close-out lock serializes same-set re-entry only; cross-set parallelism on the same branch is governed by operator discipline + the deterministic gate's check_pushed_to_remote). Added a new troubleshooting entry to ai-router/docs/close-out.md Section 6, normalized three pre-existing .close.lock references in close-out.md to match the actual code constant .close_session.lock, and added TestScenario7CrossSetParallelRejection in test_failure_injection.py that creates a real bare-remote push race with two clones and asserts the loser's gate fails non-fast-forward. All 670 Python tests pass; cross-provider verification VERIFIED (Round 2, GPT-5.4) after fixing four Round-1 issues (Q2 lead, lock filename consistency, test scope claim, Session 1 actuals visibility in the verification prompt).

reason.specifics: Session 3 (D-2) hard-scopes or removes the --force flag on close_session. Surface spans Python (close_session args + closeout_force_used ledger event), TypeScript (Session Set Explorer force-closed badge), docs, and a new test. Multi-language + careful contract wording on a security-relevant flag — Opus high-effort matches the stakes.
